rajesh.triggerSummary: HALT: Triple whammy AKI risk. Patient on RASi + SGLT2i + diuretic with illness/vomiting.
rajesh.recommendedAction: Pause SGLT2i and diuretic. Urgent eGFR + creatinine within 48 hours. If confirmed AKI: hold all three agents until renal function recovers.

rajesh.triggerSummary: HALT: Euglycemic DKA risk. Patient on SGLT2i with nausea/vomiting. Glucose may be NORMAL despite ketoacidosis.
rajesh.recommendedAction: Hold SGLT2i immediately. Check blood ketones urgently. If ketones elevated: emergency department. If no symptoms: hold 48h, resume after trigger resolves.

rajesh.triggerSummary: PAUSE: Thiazide-associated glucose rise. FBG increased 28 mg/dL in 14 days.
rajesh.recommendedAction: Consider: CCB substitution, dose reduction, or SGLT2i addition.
